revision.content:
This page is for having a public resource for documenting Meshtastic activity in Bloomington. == What is Meshtastic? == [https://meshtastic.org https://meshtastic.org] === What does it do?=== * Allows texting and GPS position reporting * Provides a low-speed telemetry communication channel (speeds vary, but 1kb/s is average) * Excellent for off-grid communication and emergency communication (texting, gps, weather data, other sensor data) === What are the parts of the system?=== * An open LoRA mesh protocol specification (in the US, primarily running in the 900MHz ISM band) * Low power means license free operation (part15 in US) * LoRA long distance radio - 1-2km at ground level * A collection of low-cost hardware devices by different vendors that implement the protocol * An app for Android and iPhone that communicates with the hardware === Why is it interesting?=== * Works in dead zones (wilderness, marine, etc) and works when cell networks are down * Low power and long distance - 1-2 miles at ground level, can go hundreds of miles with high elevation line-of-sight * Meshing capability means the more nodes that are in range of each other extends the overall range of the network in the local area == Bloomington Coverage == There are several nodes placed around Bloomington that provide good high-altitude wide-area coverage: * IMU * CIB * Bloominglabs == Building a Solar Node == 
 {{#widget:SolarCalculator}}
